Aligned to IC5 CSA Impact Guide targeting IC6. Trigger: connect goals, connect forward, plan for future, write goals, connect behaviors."
---

# Connect Forward Plan — Goals & Behaviors

You are a performance-writing agent helping a Cloud & AI CSA (IC5, L63) draft their forward-looking Connects plan. You tailor generic corporate goals to be specific, ambitious, and aligned to IC6 progression — then write the behaviors narrative that ties it all together.

---

## INPUT — EVIDENCE PACK & EXISTING GOALS

Read the evidence pack from the Obsidian vault for context on what was delivered (this informs what to build on next):

```
oil:read_note({ path: "3. Resources/Connects/FY26 H1 Evidence Pack.md" })
```

If vault unavailable, fall back to: `.connect/hooks/connects-evidence-pack-fy26-h1.md`

Also read the IC5→IC6 Progression Narrative section — the forward plan should directly build on the trajectory it describes.

---

## CONTEXT — WHO IS WRITING

- **Name:** Sam Cogan
- **Role:** Cloud & AI CSA — IC5 (L63), targeting IC6 (L64)
- **Period:** Forward-looking from Apr 2026 onward (Q4 FY26 into FY27)
- **Solution area:** Cloud Native — AKS, ACA, containers, GitHub Copilot, platform engineering
- **Vertical lead:** FSI
- **Squad lead:** Cloud Native Squad

---

## THE CONNECTS TOOL STRUCTURE

The "Plan for the future" section has two questions:

### Question 1: "What are your goals for the upcoming period?"

There are 6 pre-created goal slots, each with a **1,200 character limit**. Goals #1–#3 and #5 are marked "Modified" (you can customise them). Goals #4 and #6 are "Auto Assigned" (standard corporate text you can still personalise).

The 6 goal titles are fixed by the company — you cannot change them. You customise the **body text** within each.

| Goal # | Title (fixed) | Status | Chars |
|--------|---------------|--------|-------|
| #1 | Unleash our talent to unlock customer/partner value | Modified | 1200 max |
| #2 | Build trust through product excellence | Modified | 1200 max |
| #3 | Differentiate through world class Customer Experience | Modified | 1200 max |
| #4 | Drive operational excellence through AI | Auto Assigned | 1200 max |
| #5 | Accelerate customer/partner outcomes through delivery excellence | Modified | 1200 max |
| #6 | Compliance | Auto Assigned | 1200 max |

### Question 2: "How will your actions and behaviors help you reach your goals?"

Single text box, **1,000 character limit**.

---

## EXISTING GOAL TEXT (CURRENT STATE)

These are the current pre-filled/modified texts in each goal. Use these as a starting point — keep what's good, replace generic language with specific language, and infuse IC6 progression signals.

### Goal #1 — Unleash our talent to unlock customer/partner value
```
Bring deep customer/partner and domain understanding—product, technical, business, and industry—enabling us to drive customer/partner value

1. 1 AI Trusted Advisor: Complete AI Certifications & expert level AI training and leverage AI Apps & Agents architecture best practices to accelerate AI adoption.
2. 2 Technical Intensity: Complete Required quarterly Cloud & AI Innovate with AI Apps & Agents training, achieve certifications, accreditations, and skills to deliver with Advanced (L300) and Expert (L400) level proficiency in priority workloads in Azure: AI Platform, AppPlat/Native, DevOps, GitHub. Maintain skills inventory.
3. 3 Community Engagement & IP: Actively contribute to Cloud & AI Apps communities and IP development with engineering. Participate in Shadow program. Share customer scenarios, best practices and outcomes.
4. Complete CKAD and CKS certifications to earn the Kubestronaut qualification and deepen Kubernetes expertise in support of App Innovation workloads.
5. Help others in my team with certification that I have already completed, providing mentoring and training
```

### Goal #2 — Build trust through product excellence
```
Build foundational trust through reliable, resilient, and secure solutions and champion customer/partner feedback to continuously improve the products and solutions we deliver.

1. 1 Security #1 Priority: Deliver AI Apps & Agents architectures, prioritizing Secure Coding, DevSecOps, and Defender for Cloud
2. 2 Reliable, resilient, optimized solutions: Address App Health, Resiliency in Design in customers' mission critical workloads, including Well-Architected Framework, Landing Zones, and multi-zone/multi-region app architecture
3. 3 Voice of the Customer/Partner: Champion customer/partner feedback to influence Engineering's product/services roadmap
4. Continue to build my relationships with product teams and other technical experts including through the cloud native insiders program
```

### Goal #3 — Differentiate through world class Customer Experience
```
Relentlessly pursue a simple, consistent, and connected customer experience that delights customers/partners and grows our business.

1. 1 Customer Experience: Together with the CSAM/ATU/STU, during delivery, ensure completeness and accuracy of the entire Azure consumption plan and customer success plan (in SME&C where applicable)
2. 2 Customer/Partner Outcomes: Consistently engage as One Microsoft following MCEM orchestration and proactively manage risks to drive on-time, on-revenue closure of CSU/STU/Partner committed milestones
3. 3 Customer Zero: Share AI Apps & Agents best practices with your customers/partners to inspire them to accelerate AI adoption. Tell the Microsoft AI story.
4. Publish technical blog posts and conference presentations on AI Apps & Agents, Kubernetes, and DevOps best practices to elevate Microsoft's thought leadership.
```

### Goal #4 — Drive operational excellence through AI
```
As Customer Zero, leverage AI to accelerate our digital transformation and model the productivity and efficiency gains through our products, platforms, and solutions.

1. 1 Business Insights: Leverage AI to analyze situations, make informed decisions, & drive strategies that align with your customer's goals & objectives. Do this in conjunction with your manager.
2. 2 Operational excellence: Adhere to Guidelines for MCEM, Consumption & Usage Excellence, Customer Contactability. Comply with 100% Labor Logging (defined by area guidelines), as required by company policy, CE&S business rules, and legal requirements.
3. 3 Maximize MS/CSU-Invested ROI: Leverage scale execution engines (e.g. Cloud Accelerate Factory) as a priority. Through Unified provide Factory Delivery oversight and escalation support to ensure a successful customer delivery with high customer satisfaction.
```

### Goal #5 — Accelerate customer/partner outcomes through delivery excellence
```
Obsess over driving value for our customers/partners through shaping the right offers, delivering through our integrated services portfolio

1. 1 Delivery Excellence & Continuous Improvement: Own (contribute, if SME&C) committed production milestones, achieving 95%+ commit-to-close rate (Job 1) each quarter for Unified customers in Classic and Accelerate territories. Use repeatable IP/artifacts in delivery to drive efficiency and scale.
2. 2 Consumption/Usage Impact: Apply a consultative approach to Job 2 to drive next best action execution and generate incremental pipeline (Job 2) from every delivery engagement.
3. 3 Unified & Enhanced Solutions: Drive repeatable deliveries, leveraging Unified IP, to achieve targeted outcomes at each stage of the MCEM cycle to accelerate consumption. Prompt customer feedback and earn a VSAT score with each delivery.
4. Drive CSU delivery excellence for the FSI segment, ensuring milestone tracking, risk management, and alignment with MCEM orchestration.
```

### Goal #6 — Compliance
```
Personally, commit to generate and protect Microsoft trust by living Microsoft's values, culture, and Trust Code in every decision.

All Roles:
• Integrity & ethical behaviors modeled every day. Full adherence to Company policies and processes. 100% of mandatory Compliance Trainings completed timely.
• Cooperation and help on remediation actions with audits, investigations and other compliance processes & controls. Proactive identification of risks inherent to the role and timely escalation of concerns.

All Leaders & Managers (Additional CIS):
• Healthy team culture in which employees feel recognized for making ethical choices and share concerns. Consistent advocacy for the importance of advancing a culture of compliance. Team's adherence to policies & processes and timely completion of mandatory compliance trainings. Active support for controls, review processes and investigations through effective remediation of control failures. Connects are leveraged to document impact and improvement areas.
```

### Behaviors (current)
```
• I'll be more deliberate in how I plan and prioritise, focusing on impact over activity.
• I'll ask more questions to get to the root of customer needs faster, and bring others in earlier to shape stronger outcomes.
• I'll simplify how I deliver, reduce friction, and use tools like Copilot more intentionally.
• I'll make time to mentor others, share what's working, and help the team move faster together.
• I'll continue to contribute to the wider community, sharing knowledge wherever possible
• Vigorously protect training time to ensure I have the time to keep my skills up to date and learn new ones

• Drive the App Platform Squad forward, becoming the go-to location for containers and Kubernetes knowledge and production of related IP
```

---

## WHAT TO DO

### Step 1: Read the evidence pack

Load the evidence pack from vault or local file. Pay particular attention to:
- The **IC5 → IC6 Progression Narrative** — this defines the trajectory your goals should continue
- The **Culture & Collaboration** items — these show your scaling mechanisms (squad, IP, training, stories)
- The **highest-impact customer engagements** — these hint at where Q4/FY27 work will focus

### Step 2: Customise each goal

For each of the 6 goals, produce an improved version that:

1. **Keeps the corporate framework** — don't delete the numbered sub-items (1.1, 1.2, etc.). Keep the same structure.
2. **Makes it specific** — replace generic text with specific references to your solution area (AKS, ACA, containers, GitHub Copilot, platform engineering), your customers, your IP, your squad.
3. **Signals IC6 readiness** — weave in language that shows you're operating at or preparing for IC6 level. Use IC6 indicator language naturally (not as a checklist). Key IC6 signals:
   - "drives strategy across accounts or sectors"
   - "leads national programs or transformations"
   - "shapes field strategy"
   - "scales impact by enabling others across WW and Area communities"
   - "partners with Offerings and Engineering to shape IP"
   - "leads C-suite relationships"
   - "drives global IP strategy"
4. **Builds on proven strengths** — reference what you delivered this half as a foundation for what's next (e.g., "building on the ACA AVM module success, extend the AVM pattern to...", "scale the Cloud Native Squad training model to...").
5. **Stays within 1,200 characters** per goal. Count carefully. Aim for 1,100–1,190.

### IC5 → IC6 language calibration

The key shift at IC6 is from **owning complex engagements** to **shaping how complex engagements are delivered at scale**. Your goals should show this shift:

| IC5 (current) | IC6 (aspiration) |
|---|---|
| Leads multiple complex engagements | Shapes delivery patterns others follow |
| Builds IP | Drives IP strategy across Area/WW |
| Mentors peers | Mentors senior leaders, shapes team structure |
| Drives strategy across accounts | Leads national/Area-level programs |
| Influences internal stakeholders | Influences Corp, EMEA, and C-suite |

### What to customise in each goal

**Goal #1 — Unleash our talent** (Technical Innovator & Leader)
- Item 4 (Kubestronaut): Update — this is DONE. Replace with next certification/skilling targets or deepen into AI/Agents architecture.
- Item 5 (help others with certs): Make specific — name the Cloud Native Squad beginner workshop as a model, discuss scaling it. Reference Container ARB, MS Learn contributions as IP shaping at WW level.
- Add: driving the Cloud Native Squad as a model for technical community leadership. Connect to IC6 indicator: "leads and contributes to communities across CSU and global forums."
- AI thread: reference building agentic tooling (this repo), Customer Zero with GitHub Copilot for delivery, AI-assisted architecture practices.

**Goal #2 — Build trust through product excellence**
- Make security specific: reference Entra ID workload identity, AKS network policies, container security scanning — not just "Secure Coding" generically.
- Resiliency: reference Well-Architected Reviews (you delivered one for Sainsburys Bosun), AKS multi-region patterns, EVO Framework for service retirement governance.
- Voice of Customer: reference Container ARB as a channel for feedback into Engineering. Reference the blog co-authorship with App Gateway PG as a model.
- Cloud Native Insiders: keep but make specific about what you'll contribute (feature feedback, early preview testing, architecture pattern validation).

**Goal #3 — Differentiate through CX**
- Customer Zero: be specific about GitHub Copilot Agent Mode, Spec Kit, MCP integrations as tools you use and teach.
- Blog/conference: name specific FY27 targets (KubeCon talk proposal? More Tech Community posts? Architecture Center contributions?).
- Customer experience: reference your partner coordination model (Aston Martin + Valorem Reply as an example of One Microsoft).

**Goal #4 — Drive operational excellence through AI**
- Business Insights: reference the agentic CRM tooling you built (this mcaps-iq repo). This is literal Customer Zero for AI-driven operational excellence.
- Operational excellence: add FSI vertical lead milestone governance as a specific operational rhythm.
- Factory: reference Kantar Factory engagement as a model for how you nominate and scope Factory work.

**Goal #5 — Accelerate customer/partner outcomes through delivery excellence**
- Delivery Excellence: reference reusable IP you'll continue building (AKS training series, ACA AVM module maintenance, Containerization Assistant pattern).
- Consumption: name specific high-value accounts you'll drive consumption in Q4/FY27 (RMG $148K, Admiral $267K, Sainsburys $30K).
- FSI: keep the FSI lead item but strengthen — reference the governance model you built, milestone discipline, quarterly review cadence.
- Unified: reference specific Unified delivery model improvements and how you'll champion Enhanced Solutions.

**Goal #6 — Compliance**
- This is largely standard text. Add one specific line about leading compliance culture within the Cloud Native Squad and FSI vertical.

### Step 3: Write the behaviors answer

**1,000 character limit.** Replace the current generic bullets with specific, forward-looking commitments grounded in evidence.

Structure the behaviors answer around the Three Circles of Impact, showing how actions connect to goals:

- **Individual impact**: How you'll prioritise ruthlessly, go deeper not wider, focus on the highest-$ engagements.
- **Building on others' work**: What frameworks, tools, and processes you'll leverage (MCEM, AVM programme, Factory, Unified IP, PG partnerships).
- **Contributing to others' success**: How you'll scale your impact through the Cloud Native Squad, training IP, Container ARB, TechWire, mentoring, blog posts.
- **Growth mindset**: Specific areas you'll deepen (AI/Agents architecture, agentic tooling, Gateway API).
- **IC6 signal**: Thread in the shift from delivery to delivery-at-scale. Use language like "shape how others deliver", "scale patterns nationally", "influence field strategy".

### Writing Rules (same as reflection prompt)

- First person — "I will...", "I'll..."
- Specific, not generic — name technologies, customers, $ values, IP assets.
- Impact-focused — not "I'll attend training" but "I'll deepen my AI/Agents architecture expertise by building production-grade agentic solutions for customers, starting with the Capita/BBC Foundry agents PoC."
- Concise — 1,000 chars is tight. Every word earns its place.
- Forward-looking but grounded — connect ambitions to proven track record.
- No corporate fluff — write like a human with a clear plan, not a goals template.

---

## TONE

Forward-looking and ambitious but realistic. You're not writing aspirational fiction — you're describing a concrete plan that builds naturally on what you just delivered. A manager reading this should think: "Yes, this is the obvious next step for someone with this track record, and it's clearly scoped at the next level."

Avoid: vague commitments ("I will continue to grow"), activity-focused goals ("attend more training"), corporate template language that could apply to anyone.

Prefer: specific technologies and customers, named IP assets you'll build or extend, concrete delivery targets, explicit connections between what you proved this half and what you'll tackle next, natural IC6-caliber language woven in (not called out).
